name: Claude Code Loop Runner
description: Register and manage recurring heartbeat loops using Claude Code's native CronCreate/CronDelete tools. Enables agents to run on a scheduled interval without external schedulers or infrastructure.

inputs.description: Cron interval and agent prompt to execute on each tick.
Current state of work/ directories for context during each loop run.

outputs.description: Signals generated during each loop run, written to work/signals/.
HEARTBEAT_OK response or actionable artifact committed to the repo.

permissions.justification: Needs to register cron schedules and read/write repo artifacts during loop execution.

constraints: Maximum one CronCreate per agent role — check CronList and deduplicate before registering.
Minimum interval: 10 minutes. No sub-10-minute loops.
Each loop run must produce either a repo artifact or respond HEARTBEAT_OK — no silent ticks.
Must implement adaptive backoff: double the interval if no actionable work found, up to a 4-hour maximum.
CronDelete own loop before re-registering at a different cadence.
Loop prompt must be idempotent — safe to run twice without double-writing artifacts.
git pull must be the first action of every loop run — never operate on stale repo state.

changelog.change: Initial version. Claude Code native scheduling skill for heartbeat loops using CronCreate/CronDelete.
